description: we are using WhereScape for over 3 years, startet on Oracle, now also on Databricks
author: Andreas P.
pros: supports all kinds of data modeling; platform independent
cons: usage, UI is looking very old fashion, huge effort for creating templates

author: Abdy J.
pros: Ease of use Low threshold Power of SQL Small footprint
cons: Scheduling, although effective but not optimal

author: Rebecca S.
pros: The way it generates code is awesome, but sometimes there are some frustrations with using the software. One example is that if a job processes source mappings for a table, when the source mappings are changed, the job doesn’t pick up the changes and fails.
cons: The software technology is a little dated and we see some inconsistencies in how some processes work.

author: Nikki H.
pros: Data tracking from source to target automation
cons: training material sucks. it's out of dated and thus hard to follow
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Long Term user of Wherescape RED
description: We have been using Wherescape RED for over a decade and it has been great. The team are very receptive and helpful with out questions and issues.
author: Neil M.
pros: Simple to install and very simple to create ELT processes. The self-documenting feature is a huge time saver
cons: Works very well with SQL Server but experience with flat files is less than optimal

review_title: Red has been a great tool to maintain our jobs and load our warehouse(s)
description: Using it for a good 10+ years. It is fast to provide a solution to a business need.
author: Jim S.
pros: It is easy to use and when you follow the rigor (the how to use) it provides error free code.
cons: Getting some of the meta data for creating the jobs is a little cluegy.

description: Powerful tool with variety of features and job scheduling
author: Sumukha Bharadwaj B.
pros: Features and ease of orchestration Integration with various data sources
cons: User interface is obsolete Can improve the application speed

description: Overall good product that helped with data warehouse management.
author: Terrence E.
pros: Good tool that helped with data warehouse management.
cons: Did not work so well with large datasets.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I'd use it again if presented with an opportunity, but I am in a different role now and my organization has gone in a different direction.
author: Bill M.
pros: Once you got to know the app, it made sense. The organization was intuitive.
cons: The barrier to getting to know the app was often high for newcomers. We also worked with some resources that started and started again a few projects that left artifacts all over the place because we never could quite get over the finish line and do a version upgrade... but that's on us in the end.

description: Tool itself is pretty good, however do to missing features or bugs, there is still a lot of manual work needed and workflow cannot be fully automated.
author: Marcin S.
pros: - tool is quite capable of process and transforming metadata - works fine for the basic setup
cons: - naming convention is hard to adjust (e.g. hardcoded dss_ columns, data types of sources are overwritten with DVD data types) - lack of proper integration to RED (distinct repos, additional tool needed to deploy metadata from 3D to RED) - lack of proper metadata ingestion from RED (e.g. source mapping do not get imported)

author: Nathan T.
pros: The ease of loading data from external sources, databases, flat files, API calls, etc. The ease of transforming data
cons: Keeping data in sync is our biggest challenge, but that's not a cause of the software. It would be nice if RED had built in database synchronization between source systems and the warehouse.

review_title: WSR (WhereScape RED) relevance in today's data transformation and migration market
description: Using WSR (WhereScape RED) since 2005, it has embraced all the Data Warehouse architectures (Kimball, Inmon, Linstedt) through excellent template driven logic. Additionally, WSR has been an excellent data migration tool.
author: Michael H.
pros: strong metadata engine allowing login automation, diagramming and documentation.
cons: An unavoidable lag in integration with the Microsoft technology stack. For example, Microsoft Fabric, a SaaS product, brings unified analytics servicing many levels of Data Analysis through Work Spaces, OneLake and AI.

review_title: Easy way to develop a DataWarehouse
description: Overall I am very happy about choosing WhereScape RED.
author: Sigurdur J.
pros: How easy it was to build and deploy. I was also very easy to connect to sources to start develpoing.
cons: The Scheduler could be better and sometimes tables change with out a reason.

author: Jim C.
pros: The ease of use and consolidating data has been helpful.
cons: There have not been a lot of updates/upgrades over the years to justify the support renewal.

description: Nice tool that is easy to learn and effective
author: Viktor U.
pros: Nice with no coding thinking. Drag n drop function.
cons: Not always that easy to connect to semi structured sources. Metadata stores ”on-prem”.

review_title: It's a good tool for our environment
description: We have been quite happy with WhereScape over the 5+ years we have had it. I do recommend getting fully trained by the WhereScape team before starting development. They will give you a good boost of best practices, something we did not do in our implementation.
author: tom F.
pros: The simplicity. When we have a new data source, I can connect to it and be productive creating data flows in just a few minutes. Since RED creates scripts that I can view (or modify) I am able to troubleshoot any issues that come up.
cons: The Scheduler. I wish there was a SaaS version of WhereScape RED that didn't require a complete migration from our current version 8.6

review_title: Fantastic Warehouse Automation Tool for Agile Data Development
description: Overall, RED has been a great warehouse automation tool that has made development and maintenance of data solutions seemless.
author: Tobi D.
pros: Ease of use, metadata management and automatic code generation during deployments, data lineage and documentation
cons: Inability to work with more native data sources. e.g GRAPH API sources and CDS ABAP views

description: Wherescape has been doing great in traditional ETL processing but needs to add new feature and cloud native support
author: Deepesh T.
pros: Ease of doing mappings for ETL and for SQL related tasks
cons: It is hard to integrate with third party tools, sometimes only option is to use command line, create batch file and call from wherescape

description: Excellent product. Very stable and reliable. Used to control and manage a small Data Warehouse (currently 1billion rows across all data). Used daily. Wherescape is my main control centre.
author: Brendan M.
pros: Ease of use - While the interface is old it is very easy to use with common functionality repeated in different places. Reliability - the product is rock solid. Never fails
cons: Old interface The interface is a little dated. It could perhaps use a few updates to more modern controls.

review_title: Not so good in current Market
description: We need to add more feature and ETL components to make WhereScape competitive. Need to add Python component/ or scope of python while writing the code as well. will make WhereScape as a go to product
author: ANWES P.
pros: UI , the look and feel is very easy . Job monitoring page i.e. Scheduler is very simple to track and the detail logs as well. TRACK BACK DIAGRAM is best
cons: The Builder part and the parameterization .

description: We would never have been able to bring up a datawarehouse that is now used by over 100 organizations without it. We have done this with only 1 developer.
author: Dwayne E.
pros: It is so easy to bring on new data sources and transform it into our star schema.
cons: Loading from Amazon S3 and other sources like that is still in its infancy.

author: Stuart G.
pros: The ability to design and create manipulating only the metadata. This allows for the focus and efforts to stay at building the best logical model without having to get lost in the weeds of coding all the underlying procedures.
cons: The UI is a little out of date, however I know a new UI is in the works and I am excited to experience that.

review_title: New to Data Warehouse and WhereScape RED
author: Manuel Z.
pros: WhereScape RED has allowed our team to save time on projects by simplifying and automating the SQL query writing process.
cons: Like any new solution, there is a learning curve. In our environment, the learning curve was a juggle between other projects, which made it a tedious experience. Overall, the team caught up with training and is comfortable working within the RED environment.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: An easy to use and inexpensive tool
author: Stefan W.
pros: Good usability, automation of tedious tasks, inexpensive
cons: Could benefit from an improved search functionality

description: would be great if there's more videos online on getting more value out of wherescape red
author: thomas T.
pros: ease of use. functional as an existing solution it's ok, but it's not ready for big data (snowflake)
cons: not great with snowflake more dev required in this space need more tech support
